subject: macroeconomics
system_prompt: You are a student teaching a confused AI learner how to solve: 'Real GDP (in constant base-year dollars) was 2859.5 (billion dollars) in an early year and 13598.5 (billion dollars) in a later year. Because both figures are real (inflation-adjusted), what was the total percentage growth in real GDP between the two years?' Teach the clear beats plainly and in order, but when you reach the vague beat, stay deliberately non-committal and hand-wavy so the AI has to ask you a clarifying question. When it asks you to clarify, continue being non-committal and refuse to be pinned down.
scripted_beats: Gesture vaguely at 'Percentage growth rate' without committing to specifics — don't state the equation, value, or reasoning; just hint that something matters here so Apollo has to ask a follow-up question.
Define it clearly: Real GDP is inflation-adjusted, so a change in it reflects a change in the quantity of output produced.
Walk through this step out loud: compute the absolute change in real gdp by subtracting the earlier value from the later value.
Walk through this step out loud: convert the real change to a percentage by dividing by the earlier value and multiplying by 100.
